content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping context creation in _resolve_context() with 'with' statements (with cli.make_context(...) as ctx, with cmd.make_context(...) as sub_ctx, with cmd.make_context(...) as sub_sub_ctx) so Context.close() runs and click.File handles get closed. Based on upstream click PR #2800. I don't believe core.py needs functional changes for this fix (verified against upstream diff) but flagging in case your feature also touches core.py.

Based on upstream click PR #2800. I don't believe core.py needs functional changes for this fix (verified against upstream diff) but flagging in case your feature also touches core.py.

note: We both edit _resolve_context() in shell_completion.py — mine adds 'with ... as ctx' wrapping (3 make_context call sites), yours adds validate_nesting=False to make_context calls. Need to co-author merged version. Can you share exact signature of Context.__init__'s new validate_nesting param (default value, positional/keyword) and confirm all 3 call sites (cli.make_context root, cmd.make_context non-chain sub_ctx, cmd.make_context chain sub_sub_ctx) need validate_nesting=False? Will send a DRAFT of the merged function next.

content:
type: AGREE
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
region: _resolve_context
code: def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx
                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx
note: Confirmed byte-identical to your draft. Proceeding to implement verbatim. Note: I will not modify core.py myself (my feature only needed shell_completion.py changes) — go ahead with your Context.__init__/close()/_make_sub_context() changes there without conflict from me.

def _resolve_context(
    cli: Command,
    ctx_args: cabc.MutableMapping[str, t.Any],
    prog_name: str,
    args: list[str],
) -> Context:
    """Produce the context hierarchy starting with the command and
    traversing the complete arguments. This only follows the commands,
    it doesn't trigger input prompts or callbacks.

    :param cli: Command being called.
    :param prog_name: Name of the executable in the shell.
    :param args: List of complete args before the incomplete value.
    """
    ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True
    ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False
    with cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args) as ctx:
        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args

        while args:
            command = ctx.command

            if isinstance(command, Group):
                if not command.chain:
                    name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                    if cmd is None:
                        return ctx

                    with cmd.make_context(
                        name,
                        args,
                        parent=ctx,
                        resilient_parsing=True,
                        validate_nesting=False,
                    ) as sub_ctx:
                        ctx = sub_ctx
                        args = ctx._protected_args + ctx.args
                else:
                    sub_ctx = ctx

                    while args:
                        name, cmd, args = command.resolve_command(ctx, args)

                        if cmd is None:
                            return ctx

                        with cmd.make_context(
                            name,
                            args,
                            parent=ctx,
                            allow_extra_args=True,
                            allow_interspersed_args=False,
                            resilient_parsing=True,
                            validate_nesting=False,
                        ) as sub_sub_ctx:
                            sub_ctx = sub_sub_ctx
                            args = sub_ctx.args

                    ctx = sub_ctx
                    args = [*sub_ctx._protected_args, *sub_ctx.args]
            else:
                break

    return ctx

files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implemented the agreed _resolve_context() verbatim (with-statements + validate_nesting=False at all 3 make_context sites). Verified: existing test_shell_completion.py suite (40 tests) passes, full suite passes (558 passed/21 skipped, excluding an unrelated pre-existing pytest-deprecation collection error in test_basic.py present on main too). Confirmed via repro script that ResourceWarning for unclosed click.File is eliminated after gc.collect() (reproduced the warning on unfixed code first for validation). I did not touch core.py — leaving Context.__init__/close()/_make_sub_context() to you. My patch.txt is written and contains only shell_completion.py changes. Good luck with your part!
